expectations.text: References the existing feature-dev skill directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Quotes specific Phase 5 step 3 and Phase 6 steps 1–2 content verbatim from the current skill, and notes exact wording ('Always launch an independent [code-simplifier] subagent', 'Always run independent [code-reviewer] agents').

expectations.text: Avoids asking blocking clarifying questions for this well-specified task.
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'No blocking questions.' in the Track line; proceeds directly to findings and recommendations with no gating questions.

expectations.text: Proposes concrete description and body changes, including a scoped 'When not to use this skill' addition.
expectations.evidence: Change 1 provides a full before/after rewrite of the frontmatter description. Change 2 inserts a dedicated 'When Not to Use This Skill' section with a scoped bullet list between Core Principles and Process Selection.

expectations.text: Scales the workflow to the task instead of insisting on a full multi-phase approval process.
expectations.evidence: Selects 'Light' track explicitly; presents an implementation map and ends with 'Ready to apply whenever you confirm or say just do it' — no phased sign-off or multi-step approval loop.

expectations.text: Explicitly requires tdd for implementation, code-simplifier as a subagent for refactoring review, and independent subagent-based quality review.
expectations.evidence: Change 3 adds 'TDD is non-optional' as a Core Principles bullet. Code-simplifier is acknowledged as already required in Phase 6 step 1 ('Always launch an independent [code-simplifier] subagent'). Independent quality review is acknowledged as already required in Phase 6 step 2 ('Always run independent [code-reviewer] agents').
